title: Is there a way to default the calendar widget to a weekly view of multiple weekly filtered calendars?
excerpt: I created a new view on a board for a set of weekly calendars that are each focused on different marketing channels such as blogs, emails, and socials launching that week. Therefore on the right side, I chose the “Week” …

posts.author: jbridges-idtdna (Jennifer Bridges)
posts.cookedHtml:
I created a new view on a board for a set of weekly calendars that are each focused on different marketing channels such as blogs, emails, and socials launching that week. Therefore on the right side, I chose the “Week” view - however that view only seems to stick for me. Is there any way I can have that “Week” view automatically chosen for all other viewers as well? Or is there another way I can set this up to have a weekly calendar view of what’s launching by channel (on the board, channel is set up by a status column). In the View’s main filter, I’ve chosen “Launch Date is This Week.” And then I’ve grouped channels together such as groups of blogs, collaborations, emails, etc.

monday-weekly-view-question-hdn2213×919 101 KB

posts.author: Claudia413 (Claudia E)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Jennifer,
I haven’t found a way to do that with monday’s own calendar widget. We had a similar need and decided to include it on our own app Calendar Plus. The calendar view remembers what view you have chosen (week, or month or year) so it would be a ‘set once and forget’ way to get the week view for anyone in your team who wants to have a look. This feature is free for anyone to use forever.

You also asked about other ways to set up the calendar, from your explanation I feel we have a similar need for overview of what’s going live on what platform this week. I have made a quick demo of our setup which we ourselves use in Calendar Plus:

I color my calendar items either by platform (fb, insta, linkedin etc) or by status (seeing which ones are done and ready to be published). When coloring by status I can still see the platform in each calendar item by label.

We use the ‘information at a glance’ feature for this, which is a pro feature.

You can try the pro trial at no cost or fall back to the free version for the week view and the coloring per platform.

monday.com

Calendar Plus - monday.com App Marketplace

monday.com's most customizable calendar

Here is an example of what my calendar looks like with those settings:

posts.author: jbridges-idtdna (Jennifer Bridges)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi Claudia, your solution is an elegant way to handle this. It’s a shame that monday doesn’t have it built in but this works for what we’re looking for just dandy. Thank you for the help!!
